handoff.salientSummary: Implemented the Tauri managed multi-window layer. The Rust window manager can open settings, chat, widgets, caption, notice, about, onboarding, devtools, beat-sync, inlay, and dashboard windows with stable labels and hash routes. Secondary windows render nonblank shells, and notice/widgets flows now have Tauri-backed Eventa contracts and state registries.
handoff.whatWasImplemented: Added apps/stage-tauri/src/window_manager.rs with managed window specs, route normalization, query encoding, stable hash-suffixed child labels, reuse behavior, and tests. Added window route classification in apps/stage-tauri/src/window-routes.ts and nonblank secondary shells in App.vue. Implemented stage_tauri_managed_window_open plus Eventa-backed settings/chat/devtools/onboarding open handlers. Replaced notice placeholder commands with a pending-request registry, oneshot resolution, page-mounted/action/unmounted handling, generated ids with sequence suffixes, and a native close/destroy fallback that resolves false. Replaced widget placeholder commands with a registry-backed add/open/hide/remove/clear/update/fetch/prepare flow, native Tauri event emission, generated ids with sequence suffixes, and generation-guarded ttlMs cleanup. Added stage window contracts in @proj-airi/tauri-eventa and mapped request-window factory invokes to Tauri command names.
handoff.whatWasLeftUndone: Per-window runtime screenshots were not captured in this headless session. Secondary settings/chat/about/dashboard/etc. pages currently render minimal nonblank shells; full renderer page migration remains in later renderer-migration features. Beat-sync is represented as a visible /beat-sync shell, not the original hidden Electron beat-sync.html background window. Overlay click-through remains in later hitbox/click-through features.

commandsRun.observation: vue-tsc and Vite production build passed with existing DuckDB browser externalization, import.meta IIFE, plugin timing, and large chunk warnings.
13 adapter tests passed, including request-window invoke mapping.
tsdown build passed with stage-windows contract entry.
Rust formatting passed after final widget/notice fixes.
11 Rust tests passed with the known placeholder dead-code warnings.
Rust build passed with the known placeholder dead-code warnings.
Reviewer found notice native-close and widget ttlMs issues; both were fixed. Follow-up read-only review reported no Critical, Important, or Minor findings for those fixes.

interactiveChecks.result: Previously confirmed manually by the user before this feature completion; this feature did not regress the renderer build.

tests.coverage: Tests cover managed window labels/routes, overlay flags, route query encoding, stable child labels, route classification, notice pending/action/id behavior, widget id/generation behavior, and request-window invoke mapping.

discoveredIssues.description: Notice openWindow could hang forever if the native titlebar/OS closed a decorated notice window; fixed by resolving the pending oneshot as false on CloseRequested/Destroyed.
Widget ttlMs was stored but not enforced; fixed with generation-guarded async TTL cleanup that closes the widget window and emits the remove event.
Generated notice/widget ids were millisecond-only; fixed with process-local atomic sequence suffixes.
Dynamic child labels could collide after sanitization; fixed with stable hash suffixes.
Per-window screenshots required by the validation contract were not captured in this headless session.
AGENTS.md mandates Serena/jcodemunch MCP tools; they were not exposed in this session, so native tooling fallback was used and recorded.

deviations.step: AGENTS.md MCP startup and delegated code-change loop
deviations.whatIDidInstead: Used native rg/sed/git/apply_patch/pnpm/cargo tooling and codex exec reviewer subagents.
deviations.why: The MCP tools named by AGENTS.md were not exposed in the current tool list.
